Title: Engineering 2-Pyrone-4,6-Dicarboxylic Acid Production Reveals Metabolic Plasticity of Poplar

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in bark and xylem tissues; 3- to 5-fold upregulated in PDC transgenic lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavoring agent; reported anticancer, antiobesity, antidiabetic, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem; bark; cell wall/lignin-associated fraction; suberin in bark and roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes conversion of protocatechuic acid to vanillic acid and contributes to VA accumulation in engineered poplar
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression analysis; metabolite profiling; inference from correlated VA accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OMT21 was the only tested O-methyltransferase homologue strongly induced in PDC-pathway poplar. 2. Its expression increased 3- to 5-fold relative to WT. 3. It is implicated as a candidate endogenous enzyme methylating PCA to VA.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PDC pathway expression; salt stress context discussed for metabolite accumulation but OMT21 not directly tested under salt in the main text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate endogenous enzyme to enhance VA formation from PCA in future engineering
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role is implicated but not directly biochemically validated; text also refers in Discussion to a COMT1 homologue being upregulated, but Results specifically name OMT21 as the COMT homologue.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway linked to engineered PDC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream precursor supply for PCA/PDC production
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose 4-phosphate to form DAHP, increasing carbon flux into the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in stems of transgenic poplar lines; highest in line #1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Building block for biodegradable plastics and high-performance materials
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes shikimate flux and supports de novo accumulation of PCA and PDC in poplar stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in poplar; absolute qRT-PCR; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed enzyme in shikimate pathway; key precursor-supply control step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AroG* was part of the five-gene cassette enabling de novo PDC production in poplar. 2. It increased carbon flux through the shikimate pathway. 3. The enhanced flux likely contributed to hyperaccumulation of PCA and downstream PDC.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth; stem-specific transgene expression; salt stress increased final metabolite accumulation in transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with altered growth, bending stems, reduced stem length, and broad cell-wall metabolic reprogramming when co-expressed in pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream flux-control enzyme for in planta production of aromatic bioproducts; may need balancing to reduce growth and lignin penalties

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Conversion of shikimate intermediate to protocatechuic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor pool for downstream PDC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 3-dehydroshikimate to protocatechuic acid by dehydration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in stems of transgenic lines; generally high, highest in line #1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Building block for biodegradable plastics and high-performance materials
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly drives PCA formation, leading to strong PCA accumulation and supporting PDC production; also associated with downstream VA formation and lignin remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; qRT-PCR; LC-MS detection of PCA and PDC; cell-wall chemical analyses
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry enzyme into engineered PCA branch; effective step inferred from strong PCA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QsuB converted 3-dehydroshikimate to PCA in transgenic poplar. 2. PCA accumulated strongly in stems and bark, indicating robust QsuB activity. 3. PCA also fed VA formation and incorporation into lignin/suberin, and served as precursor for PDC.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress further increased PCA and PDC accumulation in transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with lignin reduction and altered wall composition when included in pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Effective enzyme for engineering PCA/PDC accumulation in woody biomass; requires downstream pathway balancing to reduce PCA buildup
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Prior QsuB-only poplar studies are discussed as context, but this paper tests it within the full five-gene pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Protocatechuate 4,5-cleavage pathway within engineered PDC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with PmdB as protocatechuate 4,5-dioxygenase to cleave the aromatic ring of PCA, initiating conversion toward PDC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in stems of transgenic lines; variable among lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Building block for biodegradable plastics and high-performance materials
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports PCA catabolism toward PDC production in engineered poplar
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; absolute qRT-PCR; LC-MS detection of PDC accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of a likely bottleneck dioxygenase complex due to low paired PmdB expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmdA was expressed in transgenic poplar as part of the PCA 4,5-cleavage pathway. 2. Together with PmdB it mediates PCA ring cleavage toward PDC. 3. Incomplete downstream conversion is suggested by high residual PCA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt-responsive promoter context may elevate expression in stress conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needs coordinated strong co-expression with PmdB to improve conversion efficiency from PCA to PDC
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion states PmdAB is an iron-dependent alpha2beta2 tetrameric protocatechuate 4,5-dioxygenase.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Protocatechuate 4,5-cleavage pathway within engineered PDC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts immediate precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with PmdA as protocatechuate 4,5-dioxygenase to cleave PCA en route to PDC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Consistently the lowest-abundance transgene across all transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Building block for biodegradable plastics and high-performance materials
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Insufficient PmdB expression likely restricts PCA-to-PDC conversion, causing PCA accumulation and limiting PDC yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Absolute qRT-PCR showing lowest transcript abundance; LC-MS metabolite profiling showing residual PCA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Likely bottleneck enzyme/subunit in engineered pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmdB transcript abundance was consistently the lowest among the five transgenes. 2. Low PmdB likely limits abundance of the PmdAB dioxygenase complex. 3. This bottleneck probably underlies incomplete PCA conversion and suboptimal PDC production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: AtC3'H promoter considered suboptimal in poplar; salt-responsive context discussed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Bottleneck contributes to intermediate accumulation and broader metabolic rewiring
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority target for promoter optimization or polycistronic co-expression to improve in planta PDC yields
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly identify PmdB low expression as a probable pathway bottleneck.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Protocatechuate 4,5-cleavage pathway within engineered PDC biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or immediate terminal conversion step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Product of PmdAB-mediated PCA ring cleavage
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes downstream conversion of the PCA ring-cleavage intermediate to PDC in the engineered pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in stems of transgenic lines; variable across lines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Building block for biodegradable plastics and high-performance materials
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to terminal formation of PDC in transgenic poplar
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; qRT-PCR; LC-MS detection of PDC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PmdC was included in the synthetic pathway and expressed in transgenic poplar stems. 2. Its activity is required for full conversion of PCA cleavage intermediates to PDC. 3. PDC accumulated only in transgenic lines expressing the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt-responsive promoter context discussed for increased product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary downstream enzyme for complete pathway assembly in woody biomass engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific catalytic assignment beyond terminal step was not detailed in the provided text.
